options.why: The C6 protocol is frozen as a reviewed reference. The next useful move is a bounded adoption probe design, not runtime-wide adoption.
Reference remains parked and available, but parking alone does not test the packet-law surface.
Important soon, but the C6 protocol should first get one bounded adoption-probe design so observation hardening has packet edges to inspect.
Forbidden here. C7 requires explicit later authorization after bounded protocol handling is tested.
Too broad. Runtime-wide enforcement is explicitly not authorized by the C6 reference closure.
